waypoints.note: Pre-invasion deployment. 91. Luftlande-Division (HQ Picauville, Generalleutnant Falley): Grenadier-Regiment 1057 east of the Merderet, 1058 west, plus Fallschirmjäger-Regiment 6 (attached). 709. Infanterie-Division (Generalleutnant von Schlieben): coastal stronglines along Cotentin including Utah frontage. This entry is a single MVP abstraction over both divisions' Cotentin operations; centroid approximates the operational center.

disputedBy.claim: Generalleutnant Falley (91. Luftlande-Division CO) was killed early on D-Day while returning to his HQ near Picauville from a Kriegsspiel in Rennes, ambushed by a 508 PIR patrol. Sources put the time anywhere from c. 03:00 to 06:00 local; Harrison and Zetterling give slightly different accounts of the location and identifying patrol.

waypoints.note: Falley killed near Picauville while returning to HQ; 91. LL command paralyzed early. Local German reaction to airborne drops fragmented into platoon-level engagements across the Merderet.
709. ID coastal positions engaged at Utah; Strongpoint W5 at La Madeleine overrun within an hour by 4th ID landings.
FJR 6 (Major von der Heydte) moves north from Carentan toward Saint-Côme-du-Mont to engage 101st Airborne. 91. LL cannot mount a coordinated counterattack.
End of MVP window: Cotentin defense fragmented; 709 strongpoints isolated; 91. LL contesting Merderet causeways but unable to push the airborne off Sainte-Mère-Église.
